title: ADR-0076 D: recurrence-driven tier escalation — an item with failure_count >= 1 routes its NEXT build to the deep tier
summary: Deep-tier audit reliably catches what balanced-tier build cannot finish on hard items; nothing escalates on retry, so hard items re-fail at the same tier. The failure counters exist (S5 chain, live since the claim rebinding: item JSON failure_count) and per-phase tier routing exists (advisor envelopes / per-agent overrides). Wire: at plan/claim time, an item with failure_count >= 1 sets the build phase tier floor to deep for that cycle (never lowers; envelope Max still clamps). Policy-configurable threshold (compiled default 1).
acceptance: RED: failure_count>=1 item → build tier deep in the resolved plan; count 0 → unchanged
RED: envelope ceiling still clamps (Max wins)
go test -race; wiring proof on the composed routing path; apicover clean
